commit.message:
Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

refactor: organize Theorems/ into 7 subdirectories

Move 44 theorem files into thematic subdirectories:
- Solvability/ (6): core equation theory (ClosedForm, Invertibility, etc.)
- Clamping/ (5): value clamping (ClampLocal, ClosedFormBox, etc.)
- Iteration/ (5): convergence and contraction (JacobiContraction, BinaryAlpha, etc.)
- Approximation/ (8): near-binary, blur fusion, compositing errors
- Grid/ (10): grid-level and canonical builder theorems
- FixedPrecision/ (6): fixed-width arithmetic theorems
- Evaluation/ (4): metrics and gradient semantics

All imports updated. No functional changes.

now imports Core.JacobiIteration and re-exports all names for backward compat.

commit.message:
Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:
  _NuGets.Managed.nuspec, _NuGets.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _NuGetsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _NuGetsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _Symbols.Managed.nuspec, _Symbols.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _SymbolsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _SymbolsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Managed/NativeAssets split with dynamic bin-packing

Instead of manually splitting _NuGets into .Managed and .NativeAssets
sub-packages, dynamically bin-pack all nupkgs into ~200 MB numbered
chunks (_NuGets.Dependencies.1, .2, .3, etc.). The parent meta-package
depends on all chunks.

This is self-balancing: as packages grow or shrink, the chunk count
adjusts automatically without any manual curation.

- Delete 8 .Managed/.NativeAssets nuspec files
- Add generic _Dependencies.nuspec template for chunks
- Rewrite nuget-special Cake target with first-fit decreasing bin-packing
- _NativeAssets handling (raw native binaries) is unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add /claims endpoint and fix wizard Thymeleaf expression

Two bugs fixed:

1. Missing GET /debts/claims endpoint (debt-service)
   The creditor portal called /debt-service/api/v1/debts/claims for all
   claim list views (recovery, hearing, zero-balance, rejected). The endpoint
   did not exist; Spring routed 'claims' to @GetMapping(/{id}), causing
   Invalid UUID string: claims and opening the circuit breaker.

   Added:
   - CreditorClaimListItemDto: superset of ClaimListItemDto and
     HearingClaimListItemDto so both list views deserialize correctly
   - DebtRepository.findClaimsForCreditor(): filters by creditorOrgId,
     lifecycleState (mapped from status param), and zero-balance flag
   - DebtService.getClaimsForCreditor() interface + DebtServiceImpl impl
   - DebtController: GET /claims before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

   Status mapping: IN_RECOVERY->OVERDRAGET, HEARING->HOERING,
   REJECTED->TILBAGEKALDT, ZERO_BALANCE->outstandingBalance=0

2. Thymeleaf 500 on opret fordring wizard (creditor-portal)
   The step indicator in all four wizard templates used 'step < currentStep'
   in a th:classappend expression. The < character is invalid in HTML attribute
   values and caused a TemplateProcessingException. Fixed by replacing < with
   the Thymeleaf comparison alias 'lt' in all four step-*.html templates.

commit.message:
Refactor combat engagement, add cross-attention module, and fix routing (#183)

* Remove danger tile penalty and move-to-safety reward

The danger tile penalty (-0.5) made melee engagement net-negative:
approaching an enemy cost more than hitting it rewarded (+0.25).
This incentivized camping (standing still, swinging sword repeatedly)
over actively pursuing enemies. See docs/specs/combat-engagement.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add entity-spatial cross-attention module

- EntityAttentionEncoder: add forward_with_tokens() returning per-entity
  transformer output + empty mask alongside pooled vector
- EntitySpatialCrossAttention: new 4-head cross-attention where entity
  tokens query spatial feature map (Q from entities, K/V from Conv2d)
  with empty-slot masking and masked mean pooling
- ImpalaCombinedExtractor: integrate cross-attention between entity
  encoder and concatenation, output grows from 335-d to 399-d
- ImpalaResNet: return feature map alongside pooled features for
  cross-attention to use
- ImpalaSharedAgent/ImpalaMultiHeadAgent: forward_with_attention returns
  both spatial and cross-attention weights; get_attention_entropy
  reports cross_attention/entropy and cross_attention/top1_weight
- Debugger: environment_bridge gets get_cross_attention_weights();
  game_view adds entity overlay mode with cycling (Combined → Heads →
  Entities); main_window delegates to cycle_attention_next/prev
- train.py TUI: display cross_attn/entropy and cross_attn/top1 metrics
- triforce.yaml: double main-circuit leg iterations for new parameters
- 13 new tests covering EntityAttentionEncoder tokens, cross-attention
  module shapes/masking/entropy, ImpalaCombinedExtractor output_dim
- cross-attention.md status updated to Implemented

Breaking change: models trained before this commit will not load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix routing: pre-collect starting room key in Dijkstra

When the agent starts in a room with an uncollected key, the Dijkstra
pathfinder now marks that key as collected in the initial state. This
prevents equal-cost detour routes that leave and re-enter the room
just to pick up the key the agent is already standing on.

Fixes objective suggesting S/E/W from room 0x53 instead of just W.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty action mask crash: fallback to all MOVE directions

Link can reach 'impossible' tile positions via sword knockback where
can_link_move() conservatively returns False for all directions. When
combined with no enemies/items (masking SWORD), the entire action mask
becomes empty and crashes training.

Instead of raising RuntimeError, unmask all MOVE directions as a
fallback. The NES will simply not move Link if the direction is truly
blocked, and training continues without interruption.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update model

* Fix unused-argument pylint warnings in action_space.py

Prefix unused parameters in _handle_empty_mask with underscores
to silence W0613 warnings.

app: add CSV output mechanism for steps, tracks, and events (#320)

* app: add CSV output mechanism for steps, tracks, and events

Add four new action classes (G4OCCTRunAction, G4OCCTEventAction,
G4OCCTSteppingAction, G4OCCTTrackingAction) that record simulation
data to three per-thread CSV files:

  g4occt_nt_steps_t0.csv   – one row per step (10 columns)
  g4occt_nt_tracks_t0.csv  – one row per track (13 columns)
  g4occt_nt_events_t0.csv  – one row per event (6 columns)

Each level is individually toggleable at runtime via G4GenericMessenger
commands under /G4OCCT/output/:

  /G4OCCT/output/setFileName <name>   (default: "g4occt")
  /G4OCCT/output/recordSteps  [true|false]
  /G4OCCT/output/recordTracks [true|false]
  /G4OCCT/output/recordEvents [true|false]

The output test (run_g4occt_output) intentionally omits the STEP file:
the test exercises the CSV output mechanism, not STEP import. A
pre-existing navigation heap-corruption in G4OCCTSolid manifests when
the process is started via fork+exec (CTest) and 2+ events are run;
this is orthogonal to the output feature. The run_g4occt_solid test
already covers STEP loading.

MT Geant4 builds write per-thread CSV files (_t0, _t1, ...) because
the CSV backend does not support ntuple merging. The output test runs
with -t 1 (one worker thread) for deterministic file naming.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: address review feedback on CSV output mechanism

- Extract output settings and /G4OCCT/output/ messenger into a new
  G4OCCTOutputConfig class owned by G4OCCTActionInitialization.  Both
  master and worker G4OCCTRunAction instances now receive a
  const G4OCCTOutputConfig* and read settings in BeginOfRunAction(),
  fixing thread-visibility in MT mode and the missing messenger in
  sequential builds.

- Cache the current EventID in G4OCCTEventAction::BeginOfEventAction()
  and expose GetEventId().  G4OCCTSteppingAction and
  G4OCCTTrackingAction use this instead of per-step/per-track
  G4EventManager global lookups, reducing overhead in high-stat runs.

- Use $<TARGET_FILE:g4occt> in the run_g4occt_output CTest command so
  the build-tree binary is found regardless of PATH.

- Replace cmake -E cat (existence-only) with a check_nonempty.cmake
  script that verifies each CSV has at least a header row and one data
  row via file(STRINGS ...).

- Update run_output.mac comment to reflect the _t0 per-thread suffix
  in the actual CSV filenames.
